title: Add Earwigs pest content and expand spider species guidance

requirements.text: Add a new Earwigs educational content component that follows the existing pest content layout patterns (cards + alerts) and provides clear, user-facing English guidance covering: (1) basic identification cues and common signs indoors, (2) where earwigs are commonly found and what attracts them (especially moisture), (3) prevention steps, and (4) what to do if found (safe clean-up/control steps, monitoring, and when to call a professional).

quotes: add ewigs and black widow spiders also include brown elcuse spider and along with the wolf spider

acceptanceCriteria: A new React component exists at frontend/src/components/pests/EarwigsContent.tsx (or an equivalent pests folder file consistent with the existing naming pattern) and renders without runtime errors.
All user-facing text in the new Earwigs content is in English.

The Earwigs content includes sections for identification, habitats/attractants, prevention, and what-to-do steps, using the same Card/Alert patterns used by other pest content components (e.g., CockroachesContent, SpidersContent).

requirements.text: Integrate Earwigs into the pest selection UI in frontend/src/components/EducationalContent.tsx across the Identify, Habitats, Prevention, and Treatment tabs, consistent with the existing pattern (selection button in the grid + conditional rendering via the selectedPest switch).

quotes: add ewigs and black widow spiders also include brown elcuse spider and along with the wolf spider

acceptanceCriteria: EducationalContent.tsx includes an 'earwigs' option in the selectedPest state union/type and default logic remains unchanged.
A new Earwigs selection button appears alongside existing pests in the selection grid and is usable on mobile and desktop.
Selecting Earwigs renders the Earwigs content component in Identify/Habitats/Prevention/Treatment when selectedPest is earwigs (and bed bug sections still render for bedbugs).

requirements.text: Expand the existing Spiders educational content (frontend/src/components/pests/SpidersContent.tsx) to include clearer, more detailed guidance specifically for Black Widow spiders, Brown Recluse spiders, and Wolf spiders. Add distinct subsections that include identification cues, typical indoor/outdoor hiding spots, safety/medical cautions, and what to do if found (including when to call a professional). Keep all user-facing text in English and consistent in tone with the existing Safety Warning/Medical Attention callouts.

quotes: add ewigs and black widow spiders also include brown elcuse spider and along with the wolf spider

acceptanceCriteria: SpidersContent includes clearly labeled subsections for Black Widow, Brown Recluse, and Wolf Spider guidance (not just a single bullet mention).
Each of the three subsections contains at least: identification cues, likely habitats/hiding spots, and recommended next steps (safe handling avoidance + when to call a professional).
The existing top-level Safety Warning and Medical Attention messaging remains present and the overall content remains readable and consistent with current styling.

requirements.text: Add and use a new static Earwig icon image asset under frontend/public/assets/generated, and use it in the Earwigs selection control via an tag with appropriate alt text, consistent with existing pest icon usage (e.g., cockroach/head-lice/spider icons).

quotes: add ewigs and black widow spiders also include brown elcuse spider and along with the wolf spider

acceptanceCriteria: A new earwig icon file exists at frontend/public/assets/generated/earwig-icon.dim_128x128.png.
The Earwigs selection button in EducationalContent uses the new icon via an tag and includes an alt value like "Earwigs".
No backend fetching is used for the earwig icon (served as a static asset).

constraints: Use English for all user-facing text added or modified.
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or any other frontend immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
Keep changes local to the frontend; do not introduce new backend canisters or external services.
nonGoals: Do not implement automated/AI-based spider identification or image classification.
Do not add new quiz questions or printable guides for Earwigs or spider subtypes unless explicitly requested.
Do not change authentication (Internet Identity) behavior.

required: Simple earwig icon illustration, clean line style, transparent background (earwig-icon.dim_128x128.png, 128x128)
